mapping.a_element: Wigner transform for describing partially incoherent optical wave dynamics
mapping.b_element: incoherence spectrum profile of the partially coherent wave field

mapping.a_element: evolution equation for the Wigner transform derived from a nonlinear Schrödinger equation
mapping.b_element: modulational instability analysis based on the nonlinear Kerr medium

mapping.a_element: Landau-like damping effect stabilizing modulational instability due to random phase fluctuations
mapping.b_element: incoherence suppressing or enhancing modulational instability depending on the spectrum profile
modulational instability of partially coherent waves in a nonlinear Kerr medium

mapping.a_element: geometrical optics approximation leading to stationary wave-fields
mapping.b_element: range of modulationally unstable wave numbers varying with incoherence degree

brief_justification: Both papers analyze modulational instability in nonlinear media using statistical descriptions of incoherent light (Wigner transform in A, incoherence spectrum in B). They share the core mechanism of examining how incoherence affects instability, with A deriving a damping-like stabilization and B showing spectrum-dependent suppression or enhancement, both grounded in nonlinear wave dynamics.
